attacking_style: Pure power sprinter who excels in chaotic finishes rather than traditional leadouts. Won Paris-Roubaix from three-rider sprint and UAE Tour bunch sprint through superior positioning in final meters, not early acceleration.
team_tactics: Bahrain provides full sprint train for intermediate sprints but struggles with final positioning. Team setup appears stronger for stage races than one-day classics where rider relies on individual positioning skills.
terrain_preference: Cobbled classics and hard, attritional races where bunch is reduced. Velodrome positioning exceptional. Struggles on even minor uphill finishes (8km climbs) and pure power sprints against top-tier sprinters.
vulnerability: Exposed on any uphill gradient in finals, even minor climbs. Respiratory issues (bronchitis 2022) caused catastrophic form loss. Positioning remains inconsistent despite improvements - 4th at intermediate sprints when leading train present.
key_patterns: Wins through superior positioning in reduced sprints, not pure speed (Paris-Roubaix, UAE Tour evidence)
Vulnerable to respiratory illness causing immediate competitive collapse (Catalunya 2022, Paris-Nice 2022 dropped)
Performs better as underdog than favorite - lost uphill sprint as European champion favorite
Intermediate sprint positioning failures despite full team support (Tour 2019, Tour 2021 documented)
Excels in attritional one-day races over traditional sprint stages (Roubaix win vs sprint train failures)
form_trajectory: negative: Sharp decline in early 2022 with respiratory issues causing him to be dropped on minor climbs and lose sprints despite European champion status. Medical assistance required post-race Catalunya.
